notes.note: Causae liberales were suits over a person's free or slave status, low-prestige work for an advocate; Caelius mocks the once-haughty Hirrus for stooping to them, and only in the slack hours after noon.
C. Claudius Marcellus, elected consul for 50 BC and so 'consul-designate' in late 51; an obstruction by him stalled the debate on the consular provinces.

notes.anchor: could not even produce a quorum
notes.note: Many senatorial decrees required a minimum attendance; Caelius reports the house could not muster enough members even to transact the business.

Cibyra, a district of Caelius's own beat bordering Cilicia, was famous for its big cats; Caelius wants Cicero to summon hunters from there and from Pamphylia to supply panthers for his aedilician games.
Feridius wanted the public lands leased to him (whose usufruct the provincial communities held) declared immunis --- exempt from the tribute owed to Rome --- a favour within a governor's discretion.

The columnarii were the idlers and hangers-on who loitered by the columns in the Forum (the columna Maenia area); Caelius insists it was the respectable voters, not the rabble, who failed to elect Favonius.
